cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage carefully to find out what the correct answer is for the question about the father being laid off. The question is asking what the father did when he was laid off. The choices are: (A) was angry with his boss (B) didn't care about it at all (C) couldn't accept the fact easily (D) was as happy as usual First, let’s look at the part of the passage where the father is laid off. The story says: "When I walked into the house after school, I found my dad at home. 'What are you doing home already?' I asked casually. 'Andrew, I was laid off today,' he answered quietly. I was sure he was joking. 'No, you weren't. Why are you really home?' Then I noticed his expression and realized he was telling the truth." This shows that when the father told the narrator he was laid off, he answered "quietly," and the narrator first thought he was joking. The narrator then noticed his expression and realized he was telling the truth. This suggests that the father's reaction was not one of anger or happiness, and that he wasn't joking about being laid off, but also that he didn't appear to be happy or indifferent about it. Later in the passage, the narrator describes how the father was affected: "My father has always been a hard worker and prided himself on his career. My father's unemployment created many changes in our lives... I began to notice how down he seemed, and how losing his job had affected his self-esteem, though he tried to be optimistic." This clearly shows that the father was affected by being laid off and was not happy about it. The father was "down" and had a hard time with the loss of his job, but he tried to be optimistic. This indicates that he struggled with the situation and had a hard time accepting the fact of being laid off. Additionally, the narrator says, "Although this was one of the worst experiences for our family..." which again indicates that the father's layoff was a difficult experience for everyone, and the father was not happy about it. Looking at the answer choices: (A) was angry with his boss – The passage doesn't mention the father being angry with his boss. He just quietly said he was laid off, and the focus was on how he was affected emotionally. (B) didn't care about it at all – This is clearly incorrect, as the father was clearly affected by the layoff. (C) couldn't accept the fact easily – This aligns with what the passage describes.
